steps.title: Identify the right triangle and sides relative to angle Q
steps.content: The figure shows a right triangle QRS with the right angle at R (marked by the square). From the diagram: $SR = 17$ is the bottom side. We need to find $QS$, which is the hypotenuse. Angle $Q$ is at the top vertex.

steps.title: Identify which side is opposite to angle Q
steps.content:
In a right triangle, we classify sides relative to a given acute angle. For angle $Q$:
  • The side opposite to angle $Q$ is $SR = 17$
  • The side adjacent to angle $Q$ is $QR$
  • The hypotenuse is $QS$ (the side we're solving for)

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio and solve for QS
steps.content: The sine ratio relates the opposite side and hypotenuse: $$\sin Q = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{SR}{QS} = \frac{17}{QS}$$ Multiply both sides by $QS$ and divide by $\sin Q$: $$QS \cdot \sin Q = 17$$ $$QS = \frac{17}{\sin Q}$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
